title: Distill censored time-to-next-stop into classifier features
summary: AFT survival models are designed for time-to-event targets with censoring, which matches laps-until-next-pit better than a plain binary label.
rationale: AFT survival models are designed for time-to-event targets with censoring, which matches laps-until-next-pit better than a plain binary label. Using predicted remaining-lap quantiles or transformed hazard as features can sharpen the decision boundary near pit windows without replacing the current classifier.
implementation_hint: Create a fold-safe auxiliary target equal to laps until the next pit stop, right-censor terminal stints at race end, fit an out-of-fold XGBoost `survival:aft` model, and feed its median/upper-quantile remaining-life predictions or derived next-lap event probability into the main tabular model.
expected_effect: Better ordering around ambiguous mid/late-stint examples where simple tyre-life thresholds are too coarse.
risk: Easy to leak future information when constructing time-to-event labels or race aggregates; all survival predictions must be strictly out-of-fold and sequence-causal.
